name: go-mcp — Go MCP SDK
what_it_does: Go MCP SDK (mcp-go) providing a complete Model Context Protocol implementation for Go developers — enabling building type-safe MCP servers in Go with tool registration, resource management, prompt handling, and MCP transport layer support, making Go a first-class language for MCP server development.
use_cases: Building high-performance MCP servers in Go from backend teams
Creating memory-efficient MCP integrations for existing Go services
Exposing Go microservice functionality as MCP tools from platform teams
Building concurrent MCP servers with Go's goroutine model from infra teams
Creating typed, safe MCP server implementations from SDK-first teams
Integrating MCP into Go CLIs and system tools
not_for: Non-Go developers (use Python or TypeScript MCP SDKs)
Consuming MCP servers (this is for building them)
Simple scripts — Go compilation overhead not worth it for simple one-off MCPs
best_when: A Go developer or team needs to build a high-performance, concurrent MCP server — particularly for integrating existing Go services or building infrastructure tooling.
avoid_when: You don't use Go or need the most mature/supported MCP SDK — use the official Python or TypeScript MCP SDKs.

auth.notes: Go SDK — no auth required to use the library. Auth implementation in built servers is developer responsibility.

pricing.notes: Free, open source Go module. Available via go get.

performance.rate_limits: Local library — performance determined by Go runtime and built server logic

known_agent_gotchas: SDK for building, not consuming — cannot be used as an MCP server directly
Go module versioning — use go.mod to pin versions for stability
MCP spec version compatibility — check SDK release notes for spec support
Community SDK (mark3labs) — not official Anthropic Go SDK; well-maintained with good adoption
Go compilation required — adds build step compared to interpreted language MCPs
Concurrency patterns in Go require careful resource management for MCP tools
agent_readiness.security_notes: SDK framework — developer implements security. No SDK-level credentials. Follow Go security best practices. TLS implementation is developer responsibility for remote MCP servers.

security_score_components.security_notes: SDK framework. Developer implements auth/TLS. Well-maintained Go module. Follow Go security best practices.
